lessons.title: General Principles of EU Law
lessons.description: We have examined the written sources of EU law — the treaties, the Charter, and secondary legislation. But EU law also has an important category of unwritten law: the general principles.

lessons.title: Fundamental Rights — Development and the Charter
lessons.description: In Lesson One, we saw that fundamental rights form part of the general principles of EU law.

lessons.title: Scope of the Charter — When Does It Apply to Member States?
lessons.description: We know that the Charter of Fundamental Rights is legally binding from 1 December 2009. We know it applies to EU institutions and bodies at all times. But when does it apply to Member States?
